title: The High Country Render

features.name: wireframe ridge
features.desc-on-touch: the mountain rendered as coordinate lines, a landscape turned inside-out into pure geometry. you climb its intersection points.

features.name: bushranger silhouette
features.desc-on-touch: a figure holding what might be a jetpack or might be the landscape's own gesture. when you look directly it phases between both.

features.name: colour-reef
features.desc-on-touch: bands of impossible hues stacked like strata, the archive's render finding colours the earth never learned it needed.

features.name: diagonal-glyph-rows
features.desc-on-touch: marks that might be text or landscape contours, angled like the earth is leaning away. the orchard pattern has become a message.

features.name: cloud-arch
features.desc-on-touch: the neon overcover rendered as a doorway, or the sky is shaped like a gate. the ascent continues upward through its opening.

features.name: horizon-pool
features.desc-on-touch: luminous liquid pooling at the render's edge, the place where colour and space meet and remember they are not the same thing.

inscription: the earth learned it could be colour before it learned it could be still

file.name: High_Country_Projection_Quaternary_Vector_Space.txt
file.note: topographic data corrupted into spectral coordinates; the mountain exists as hue gradient and angle

exits.label: the jetpack settling back toward fluorescent palms
deeper into colour, where the render meets the edge of sight
the ridge continuing eastward, becoming less geometric with every step
upward into the cloud-arch, the neon overcover descending to meet you
exits.to: NEW: the threshold where wireless signal becomes weather
